title: Ritsukos emotional state after the SEELE incident
display_title: Ritsukos emotional state after the SEELE incident
wikitext:
[]: Ritsuko's emotional state after the SEELE incident             Quote Post by lilim » Fri Nov 19, 2010 4:01 pm i just finished reading this thread , but i was wondering if there were any more recent discussions on the topic? or maybe we could discuss here? as i was reading through, i wanted to talk about the theories that Ritsuko was basically just jealous and pissed and wanted to kill Rei, and moreso, maybe hoping she could bully Shinji into killing her . i hope making a new thread on it is an alright way to go about this, and that maybe someone would like to discuss it with me. but anyway, here goes. i never got that impression when i watched Eva, i think if she had wanted to kill Rei she would have destroyed the dummy plugs and gone on to murder Rei 3. she says she hates them because they're human shaped things, and because she lost to them despite that. that doesn't really imply that she hates Rei, she IS jealous, but it's the difference of: i lost my lover to a schoolgirl/i lost my lover to a doll. the first is a revolting thought certainly worthy of hatred and rage and jealousy but the latter is much more degrading and humiliating. she didn't lose to a younger woman. she lost to an aquarium full of empty shells. she hates how Gendo treats the clones as if they're above her. i think that's a different thing than saying simply "she hates Rei." i don't think she hates Rei as a "person" and she does seem to view Rei 2 as such. because she's not just a clone, she has a soul. (and she does even say she's a nice girl.) she just becomes disgusted with the relationship Gendo has with the clones. if she truly hated Rei, like i said, then destroying the DPS would be only half of her revenge. she would surely want to kill Rei 3 herself as well. i think her revenge on Gendo has more to do with his work. when i first watched it, i got the feeling that she brought Shinji down there to turn him on his father. i think the theory that she also wanted to show him the truth, which she was denied in that telling flashback, also makes sense as part of the reason to bring him with her. but i do not think it was to kill Shinji, or to get Shinji to kill her, or even to have him kill Gendo..granted, i have thought Shinji had a sadistic side under the surface, in response to all the things in his life that have been so traumatic, it's probably normal to have a rage inside you, but he hides it well and is, imo, a fundamentally good person. the reason i think the "Ritsuko expected him to kill her on her own orders" thing makes no sense is that Ritsuko knows, or should know, that he doesn't really just take any order he's given--think about how he responds when Gendo wants him to kill Eva 03. granted, no one should want to blindly take orders to kill another human, even Rei had problems doing what she was commanded to do (if she hadn't paused, thinking "he's in there.." i doubt she would have been attacked) but Shinji, more than most characters in that show, has an extremely mild, passive personality and i doubt very highly that he'd just accept Ritsuko's request and kill her. if her plan consisted of him being filled with murderous rage, it would more logically be directed at Gendo, not her, he'd have no reason to shoot the woman who clued him in on all that's been going on (or at least a good chunk of it, with the DPS) but either way i can't see her really believing that Shinji would actually murder his father after she does what she does. who could really see Shinji murdering ANYONE? "just reason" or not, he's not that kind of character and she knows this. to me, her taking him down there was a desperate grand gesture. she was prepared to make a theatrical performance, enlighten him on a good deal of the underbelly of Nerv's operations, and hopefully have someone to commiserate with on how horribly violated they both should be after what Gendo has done. maybe she was simply hoping to project some of her pain on Shinji, her thinking along the lines of "you're his child, you should feel this terrible betrayal too. i'm not the only one he makes suffer." (remember the line "Shinji and Rei wouldn't forgive us if they found out"? maybe she was just hoping to cash in on that theory?) it's almost like a stepmother hiding her stepson from her husband's lecherous, shady ways, but then when the husband turns on her, she rats him out to the son. "look what he did to us". and about her wanting to kill Shinji, what could she possibly get out of that? if she was crazy enough at that point to kill him just for the satisfaction of killing Gendo's child, she'd have taken Misato's gun and killed him right there anyway. i just can't see her wanting to do that. she's not murderous, she's just broken and sad. also, i don't think she wanted Shinji to kill her, at all. she isn't suicidal. she does make the comment to Misato "you can kill me if you want. no, i'd be happy if you did" but i think it's really just a cry for help at that point, rather than a real desire to die. at the same time i don't believe that "if she wanted to die she'd do it herself"; there is a possibility that she wanted to die but not in the same way her mother did. maybe she wanted to be a martyr. "i was used and broken by Gendo and i'm a disgusting woman so *insert name here* should kill me and get it over with.." it's like how some depressed or bi polar patients will say they want to die but rather than finding a way to do themselves in, they run out in front of police and taunt them, they bait them into doing it for them because for whatever reason THEY don't want to take their own life with their own hands. then again this is just what i think of her at this point in the series, her self destruct plan in EoE is a different story altogether, but in the scene where she destroys the DPS i really think it was more like a combination of things: -she'd been depressed and unstable and completely violated after what happened with SEELE. and i'm led to believe, since she'd made comments previously, like in conversation with Maya about the DPS, that she views herself as someone who started out with a "fastidious nature" and tried to maintain that front for a long time, despite her suppressed feelings of being "sullied" by Gendo, and perhaps by Nerv as a whole. assuming she was raped, it makes more sense that that was the thing that broke her since they elude to that kind of "i've been soiled" attitude earlier on, but she definitely avoided these feelings and denied them, if she indeed did have them. (which i think she did.) to me, it's like when Misato criticizes Shinji for overthinking Asuka's reaction ("I wonder why she got so mad" "Does that bother you?" "Yes, it does" "That's because all you ever do is worry about what other people think.") SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Sun Nov 21, 2010 8:17 pm Wow, honestly I'd never really given the final scenes with Ritsuko much thought. I was usually too preoccupied with everything else going on at the time. Personally I think of Ritsuko as one of the most outright depressing characters of the series. This is probably because the final revelations about her character and her background initially clash with the calculating and collected persona she always portrays. Perhaps she, above all other characters, has the wherewithal to say "Fuck it" at the end, which she does, but it turned out to be too little too late. As you said she destroyed the Rei clones, but didn't kill Rei III, ultimately accomplishing nothing (other than revealing to Shinji the secret of the Reis). She appears with pistol drawn as Gendo prepares to finalize his dream of the past decade, but she doesn't shoot him or really show any intention of shooting him. The fact is that she loves Gendo (for whatever weird reason) but it is painfully obvious that he doesn't reciprocate her feelings. Nevertheless she makes no attempt to shoot Gendo, but in fact she almost puts her own plan into action to kill both her and Gendo in what could be considered her plot to bring them together forever. The appearance of Ritsuko prior to the reunion of Rei and Lilith implies that she knew what Gendo's plan was, and how he planned to act it out. So the fact that she knew the whole time that Gendo's plan was to "reunite" with Yui, makes her story even more sad and her breakdown at the end even more understandable. What's even more weird is the fact that her mother had an identical relationship with him years before, and it ended with a murder suicide! And she still got together with him?! I dont get it, Gendo must have it goin on or something. In the end I'm not really sure what, if any, were her specific intentions other than just blowing up everything and saying "to hell with it," but it's at least obvious that she saw that the end of Gendo's plan was coming and didn't want to be around to see it. Well, she wasn't. As for her intentions behind taking Shinji to see the Rei clones before she killed them... a little bit of sadism maybe? By the end she doesn't really seem to care about anyone's well being, not that she really needs to since people are dropping like flies, but it drives the point home even more when you consider everything that's going on around her and how detached she is about it. She's kind of like Shinji in this respect, she's so wrapped up in her own emotions that she can't or isn't willing to face reality. Again, it's not like she has much of a reality to face. Even the ones that survived until Instrumentation weren't given much of a choice. Really the only choice at the end was death or being tanged. What I think is ironic is how she dislikes Rei and considers her to be her "rival" and in the end when she got shot Rei was the last thing she saw, and the look of surprise on her face is clearly shown, before she instantly died upon hitting the water (it's easier than the realistic splashing and moaning of a person who's bleeding to death in a reservoir of menstrual fluids). That'd be like being in the KKK all your life and then going to heaven and finding out that God is black, and Jesus was middle eastern.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 22, 2010 1:08 am So Jimbo beat Xard once and for all by reincarnating as a teenaged girl, eh? Impenetrable walls of text aside, I will comment that I don't think Ritsuko EVER new the real truth except perhaps in Instrumentality. She probably has no idea why her mother killed herself because she doesn't know who Rei is a clone of, and thus probably thinks Gendo is just trying to become a god (as in the manga) rather than that this was all a ridiculously elaborate attempt to end his decade-old marriage separation.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Mon Nov 22, 2010 4:05 pm Hahah don't think that I'm so sure of myself just cuz I write a lot, I'm just putting all my mind-ejaculations into type.

[]:             Quote Post by lilim » Mon Nov 22, 2010 6:31 pm i'm sure that was directed at me, since i posted WAY more than i should have ^^; which makes me want to thank both of you for responding in the first place. i realized after i posted the thread that i should have reigned myself in some but i'm not very good at that. i don't know much about Gendo in the manga, since i just started reading and am not very far into it yet, but that's interesting. but i would think she would have some idea of what happened with her mother. it would be kind of weird if she didn't. and this.. Personally I think of Ritsuko as one of the most outright depressing characters of the series. This is probably because the final revelations about her character and her background initially clash with the calculating and collected persona she always portrays.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 22, 2010 6:48 pm Really? I always sort of liked Ritsuko, just thought of her as a mostly decent person who's unfortunate attractions (and it's not completely crazy, Gendo is after all a very powerful man with intense force of personality) and the needs of the conflict led her to accept some rotten deals in life. Her detachment is just a coping mechanism for all the compromises she's had to make, not so different from Misato's drinking. As far as Ritsuko's knowledge or lack thereof, all that we have to go on is the fact that Rits doesn't get all the facts right in her 'they found a god' speech, and that it happens AFTER her 'meeting' with Seele means that we can't be sure how much of this she knew previously and how much they only recently dumped on her. Besides, if she knew from the start that Rei was a mini-Yui I don't think she'd be suprised at all at Gendo's favoritism, and her complaints in that department would tend to be more personal rather than focused on Rei's questionable status as human.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Nov 22, 2010 8:12 pm Neat insights! Though, it';s been a while since I sat and watched the whole series. I forget in what context this took place in within the series: lilim wrote: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Mon Nov 22, 2010 8:22 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Neat insights! Though, it';s been a while since I sat and watched the whole series. I forget in what context this took place in within the series:

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Nov 22, 2010 8:27 pm Ah, gotcha. My ignorance showing like this generally means I just need to rewatch the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Pixelated Anarchy » Fri Nov 26, 2010 6:52 pm Okay, so I've been following this post for a while and I've been meaning to analyze it and reply to it, but I just haven't found much time to do it, until now (Thank God). This is one of those posts that seem mundane on the surface, but when you open up to it, it's really fun to discuss and interesting, pretty much the the entire series itself. I was worried that the post might be too old to reply to, but I guess no post is ever TOO old. Anyway, as fair warning in this preamble, I am going point by point, so a few things might seem redundant. Here's my opinion on lilim's post: I didn't get the impression either that Ritsuko even desired to kill Rei. However, it's not in Ritsuko's personality to be completely belligerent. Killing Rei outright in front of everyone - or even in secret - would not have been an option for her unless she could do it without having to get blood on her labcoat. No, I think her dispatching of the clones was what she would be comfortable with. All she had to do was effortlessly press a button, and every Rei in the tank died. I consider it more in her character to use opportunity and ease to kill anyone, which is what happened in the - oh so affectionately dubbed - Reiquarium; she achieved the most by doing so little. Plus, any physical confrontation of Rei probably would have destabilized her even more than she was after the SEELE incident. This mentality of subterfuge might reopen the theory that she could have gotten Shinji to kill Rei, but I think don't think Ritsuko could have gotten him to do it because of his age, the companionship (no matter now far apart it was), the gravity of the desire, and Shinji's morals to not harm anyone makes manipulation improbable. Now, as for the reasons behind her annihilation of the clones, I can infer that it might be a combination of two issues: Ritsuko as a woman, and Ritsuko as Gendo's lover, but mostly the latter. For Ritsuko as a woman, she probably felt jealous of the clones because they were just human-shaped experiments to her, and she even says this. But the WHY of the issue more than likely comes in as Gendo's lover. It's apparent that Gendo values his work above most, if not all, people, no matter his reasoning behind it. The same thing, I think, applies to Ritsuko. She hates the clones because Gendo values them more than he does her. It's a matter of jealousy towards his work, not because he shares any relationship with these clones, or with Rei 2 for that matter (which is more of a father/ daughter thing anyway). Ritsuko is a human, and humans tend to get jealous when something interferes with the attention they desire. To explain the point, let's set up a scenario: A multi-millionaire businessman and his wife live together with immeasurable riches. They live happily together, but the businessman is away for months at a time to make his living. While the wife doesn't have an affair with anyone else and she's well off, she still hates the fact that her husband is preoccupied with his work which is essential to their lifestyle. The wife tries to keep her husband at home for as long as possible until she's tired of living without him and leaves him for someone else. The point is, despite the riches or the importance of the work, humans want the full attention of their significant other, and are willing to live with less of anything if it means they get to see their significant other more often. Ritsuko basically thought "As much as we need this work to ensure survival, I want Gendo to be with me more." I think it's even possible to argue that as level as she appeared to be, she just repressed her desires to be loved by Gendo just because she knew the importance of the work. However, after the SEELE incident, she just couldn't keep it together and she wanted to send a message to Gendo just to say "Hey, why aren't you paying attention to me?! I should be more important than your work!" But this repression also applies to anything else she does throughout Evangelion; she's very subservient to Gendo at the beginning, but as everything fell apart, Ritsuko steps out of the subservient shell and is independently acting of her own will. Now, to conclude her reasoning behind the destruction of the clones, it goes back to her being a woman. While she wanted Gendo's attention, she also was jealous that the soulless abominations took more precedence than she did. After the SEELE incident, her mentality was probably "How did these clones win out to me? I'm an actual human and they can't even compare to me. Gendo just threw me to to dogs when he could have sacrificed one of these shells to SEELE." Being further humiliated by Gendo by being sacrificed to SEELE without so much as a second glance probably set her over the edge. The killing of the clones then would have made sense, not to satisfy a desire to murder Rei, but as revenge for the importance they had over her. In my opinion, Shinji was brought into NERV not just because he was ordered to pilot Eva 01 by his father. I mean, it is the MAIN reason, because he wanted his father to approve of him, but I think every major character who understood NERV's practices had their own reason for it. Ritsuko isn't the type of person I think who would want to use Shinji to kill Gendo by inciting him, even though he may have wanted to because Gendo left him on the streets at 5. She doesn't even seem to have any intention of doing it, considering she is with him secretly for a good part of the series and could do it herself. No, other than to show NERV's practices to Shinji and to shed light on the organization, it was to attack Gendo emotionally (though this is probably a vain effort on her part). Ritsuko could send a message to Gendo emotionally by bringing Shinji to him saying "You can't just recluse yourself in your work. There are people here who care about and you know you care about and you just can't keep shutting them out.", which would work exceptionally well for Ritsuko's situation and her desired use of subterfuge to achieve her goals. I think everything else that lilim touched on I agree with, but I just thought I'd share my thoughts on the issue. But another theory that I was thinking about (though it may not make much sense) is that Ritsuko could perhaps have blamed Rei for her mother's death, even though she took her own life. I would think no matter what happened to a recently deceased sibling, anything that had to do with him or her, you automatically associate a hatred for that object or thing, because to you it brought about their death. Even though Ritsuko hated her mother and didn't want to be like her (we all know how well THAT went), she would probably feel like Rei was the reason she died, and she was old enough to have a vivid memory of her death. I'll admit that the issue seems flawed, but to some extent it makes sense because the mental trauma that came with the SEELE incident could have also released her association with Rei to her mother's death, assuming the whole mother flashback came to her after the SEELE incident and before the killing of the clones (I honestly don't remember what happened first). It's obvioulsy not canon, I just wanted to share an opinion on it.
summary: []: Ritsuko's emotional state after the SEELE incident             Quote Post by lilim » Fri Nov 19, 2010 4:01 pm i just finished reading this thread , but i was wondering if there were any more recent discussions on the topic? or maybe we could discuss here? as i was reading through, i wanted to talk about the theories that Ritsuko was basically just jealous and pissed and wanted to kill Rei, and moreso, maybe hoping she could bully Shinji

[]: Ritsuko's emotional state after the SEELE incident             Quote Post by lilim » Fri Nov 19, 2010 4:01 pm i just finished reading this thread , but i was wondering if there were any more recent discussions on the topic? or maybe we could discuss here? as i was reading through, i wanted to talk about the theories that Ritsuko was basically just jealous and pissed and wanted to kill Rei, and moreso, maybe hoping she could bully Shinji into killing her . i hope making a new thread on it is an alright way to go about this, and that maybe someone would like to discuss it with me. but anyway, here goes. i never got that impression when i watched Eva, i think if she had wanted to kill Rei she would have destroyed the dummy plugs and gone on to murder Rei 3. she says she hates them because they're human shaped things, and because she lost to them despite that. that doesn't really imply that she hates Rei, she IS jealous, but it's the difference of: i lost my lover to a schoolgirl/i lost my lover to a doll. the first is a revolting thought certainly worthy of hatred and rage and jealousy but the latter is much more degrading and humiliating. she didn't lose to a younger woman. she lost to an aquarium full of empty shells. she hates how Gendo treats the clones as if they're above her. i think that's a different thing than saying simply "she hates Rei." i don't think she hates Rei as a "person" and she does seem to view Rei 2 as such. because she's not just a clone, she has a soul. (and she does even say she's a nice girl.) she just becomes disgusted with the relationship Gendo has with the clones. if she truly hated Rei, like i said, then destroying the DPS would be only half of her revenge. she would surely want to kill Rei 3 herself as well. i think her revenge on Gendo has more to do with his work. when i first watched it, i got the feeling that she brought Shinji down there to turn him on his father. i think the theory that she also wanted to show him the truth, which she was denied in that telling flashback, also makes sense as part of the reason to bring him with her. but i do not think it was to kill Shinji, or to get Shinji to kill her, or even to have

Article: Ritsukos emotional state after the SEELE incident
 truth, which she was denied in that telling flashback, also makes sense as part of the reason to bring him with her. but i do not think it was to kill Shinji, or to get Shinji to kill her, or even to have him kill Gendo..granted, i have thought Shinji had a sadistic side under the surface, in response to all the things in his life that have been so traumatic, it's probably normal to have a rage inside you, but he hides it well and is, imo, a fundamentally good person. the reason i think the "Ritsuko expected him to kill her on her own orders" thing makes no sense is that Ritsuko knows, or should know, that he doesn't really just take any order he's given--think about how he responds when Gendo wants him to kill Eva 03. granted, no one should want to blindly take orders to kill another human, even Rei had problems doing what she was commanded to do (if she hadn't paused, thinking "he's in there.." i doubt she would have been attacked) but Shinji, more than most characters in that show, has an extremely mild, passive personality and i doubt very highly that he'd just accept Ritsuko's request and kill her. if her plan consisted of him being filled with murderous rage, it would more logically be directed at Gendo, not her, he'd have no reason to shoot the woman who clued him in on all that's been going on (or at least a good chunk of it, with the DPS) but either way i can't see her really believing that Shinji would actually murder his father after she does what she does. who could really see Shinji murdering ANYONE? "just reason" or not, he's not that kind of character and she knows this. to me, her taking him down there was a desperate grand gesture. she was prepared to make a theatrical performance, enlighten him on a good deal of the underbelly of Nerv's operations, and hopefully have someone to commiserate with on how horribly violated they both should be after what Gendo has done. maybe she was simply hoping to project some of her pain on Shinji, her thinking along the lines of "you're his child, you should feel this terrible betrayal too. i'm not the only one he makes suffer." (remember the line "Shinji and Rei wouldn't forgive us if they found out"? maybe she was just hoping to

Article: Ritsukos emotional state after the SEELE incident
 of "you're his child, you should feel this terrible betrayal too. i'm not the only one he makes suffer." (remember the line "Shinji and Rei wouldn't forgive us if they found out"? maybe she was just hoping to cash in on that theory?) it's almost like a stepmother hiding her stepson from her husband's lecherous, shady ways, but then when the husband turns on her, she rats him out to the son. "look what he did to us". and about her wanting to kill Shinji, what could she possibly get out of that? if she was crazy enough at that point to kill him just for the satisfaction of killing Gendo's child, she'd have taken Misato's gun and killed him right there anyway. i just can't see her wanting to do that. she's not murderous, she's just broken and sad. also, i don't think she wanted Shinji to kill her, at all. she isn't suicidal. she does make the comment to Misato "you can kill me if you want. no, i'd be happy if you did" but i think it's really just a cry for help at that point, rather than a real desire to die. at the same time i don't believe that "if she wanted to die she'd do it herself"; there is a possibility that she wanted to die but not in the same way her mother did. maybe she wanted to be a martyr. "i was used and broken by Gendo and i'm a disgusting woman so *insert name here* should kill me and get it over with.." it's like how some depressed or bi polar patients will say they want to die but rather than finding a way to do themselves in, they run out in front of police and taunt them, they bait them into doing it for them because for whatever reason THEY don't want to take their own life with their own hands. then again this is just what i think of her at this point in the series, her self destruct plan in EoE is a different story altogether, but in the scene where she destroys the DPS i really think it was more like a combination of things: -she'd been depressed and unstable and completely violated after what happened with SEELE. and i'm led to believe, since she'd made comments previously, like in conversation with Maya about the DPS, that she views herself as someone who started out with a "fastidious nature" and tried to maintain that front for a

 SEELE. and i'm led to believe, since she'd made comments previously, like in conversation with Maya about the DPS, that she views herself as someone who started out with a "fastidious nature" and tried to maintain that front for a long time, despite her suppressed feelings of being "sullied" by Gendo, and perhaps by Nerv as a whole. assuming she was raped, it makes more sense that that was the thing that broke her since they elude to that kind of "i've been soiled" attitude earlier on, but she definitely avoided these feelings and denied them, if she indeed did have them. (which i think she did.) to me, it's like when Misato criticizes Shinji for overthinking Asuka's reaction ("I wonder why she got so mad" "Does that bother you?" "Yes, it does" "That's because all you ever do is worry about what other people think.") SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Sun Nov 21, 2010 8:17 pm Wow, honestly I'd never really given the final scenes with Ritsuko much thought. I was usually too preoccupied with everything else going on at the time. Personally I think of Ritsuko as one of the most outright depressing characters of the series. This is probably because the final revelations about her character and her background initially clash with the calculating and collected persona she always portrays. Perhaps she, above all other characters, has the wherewithal to say "Fuck it" at the end, which she does, but it turned out to be too little too late. As you said she destroyed the Rei clones, but didn't kill Rei III, ultimately accomplishing nothing (other than revealing to Shinji the secret of the Reis). She appears with pistol drawn as Gendo prepares to finalize his dream of the past decade, but she doesn't shoot him or really show any intention of shooting him. The fact is that she loves Gendo (for whatever weird reason) but it is painfully obvious that he doesn't reciprocate her feelings. Nevertheless she makes no attempt to shoot Gendo, but in fact she almost puts her own plan into action to kill both her and Gendo in what could be considered her plot to bring them together forever. The appearance of Ritsuko prior to the reunion of Rei and Lilith implies that she knew what Gendo's plan was, and how he planned to act it out

Article: Ritsukos emotional state after the SEELE incident
 and Gendo in what could be considered her plot to bring them together forever. The appearance of Ritsuko prior to the reunion of Rei and Lilith implies that she knew what Gendo's plan was, and how he planned to act it out. So the fact that she knew the whole time that Gendo's plan was to "reunite" with Yui, makes her story even more sad and her breakdown at the end even more understandable. What's even more weird is the fact that her mother had an identical relationship with him years before, and it ended with a murder suicide! And she still got together with him?! I dont get it, Gendo must have it goin on or something. In the end I'm not really sure what, if any, were her specific intentions other than just blowing up everything and saying "to hell with it," but it's at least obvious that she saw that the end of Gendo's plan was coming and didn't want to be around to see it. Well, she wasn't. As for her intentions behind taking Shinji to see the Rei clones before she killed them... a little bit of sadism maybe? By the end she doesn't really seem to care about anyone's well being, not that she really needs to since people are dropping like flies, but it drives the point home even more when you consider everything that's going on around her and how detached she is about it. She's kind of like Shinji in this respect, she's so wrapped up in her own emotions that she can't or isn't willing to face reality. Again, it's not like she has much of a reality to face. Even the ones that survived until Instrumentation weren't given much of a choice. Really the only choice at the end was death or being tanged. What I think is ironic is how she dislikes Rei and considers her to be her "rival" and in the end when she got shot Rei was the last thing she saw, and the look of surprise on her face is clearly shown, before she instantly died upon hitting the water (it's easier than the realistic splashing and moaning of a person who's bleeding to death in a reservoir of menstrual fluids). That'd be like being in the KKK all your life and then going to heaven and finding out that God is black, and Jesus was middle eastern.

Impenetrable walls of text aside, I will comment that I don't think Ritsuko EVER new the real truth except perhaps in Instrumentality. She probably has no idea why her mother killed herself because she doesn't know who Rei is a clone of, and thus probably thinks Gendo is just trying to become a god (as in the manga) rather than that this was all a ridiculously elaborate attempt to end his decade-old marriage separation.

[]:             Quote Post by Pixelated Anarchy » Fri Nov 26, 2010 6:52 pm Okay, so I've been following this post for a while and I've been meaning to analyze it and reply to it, but I just haven't found much time to do it, until now (Thank God). This is one of those posts that seem mundane on the surface, but when you open up to it, it's really fun to discuss and interesting, pretty much the the entire series itself. I was worried that the post might be too old to reply to, but I guess no post

Article: Ritsukos emotional state after the SEELE incident
 seem mundane on the surface, but when you open up to it, it's really fun to discuss and interesting, pretty much the the entire series itself. I was worried that the post might be too old to reply to, but I guess no post is ever TOO old. Anyway, as fair warning in this preamble, I am going point by point, so a few things might seem redundant. Here's my opinion on lilim's post: I didn't get the impression either that Ritsuko even desired to kill Rei. However, it's not in Ritsuko's personality to be completely belligerent. Killing Rei outright in front of everyone - or even in secret - would not have been an option for her unless she could do it without having to get blood on her labcoat. No, I think her dispatching of the clones was what she would be comfortable with. All she had to do was effortlessly press a button, and every Rei in the tank died. I consider it more in her character to use opportunity and ease to kill anyone, which is what happened in the - oh so affectionately dubbed - Reiquarium; she achieved the most by doing so little. Plus, any physical confrontation of Rei probably would have destabilized her even more than she was after the SEELE incident. This mentality of subterfuge might reopen the theory that she could have gotten Shinji to kill Rei, but I think don't think Ritsuko could have gotten him to do it because of his age, the companionship (no matter now far apart it was), the gravity of the desire, and Shinji's morals to not harm anyone makes manipulation improbable. Now, as for the reasons behind her annihilation of the clones, I can infer that it might be a combination of two issues: Ritsuko as a woman, and Ritsuko as Gendo's lover, but mostly the latter. For Ritsuko as a woman, she probably felt jealous of the clones because they were just human-shaped experiments to her, and she even says this. But the WHY of the issue more than likely comes in as Gendo's lover. It's apparent that Gendo values his work above most, if not all, people, no matter his reasoning behind it. The same thing, I think, applies to Ritsuko. She hates the clones because Gendo values them more than he does her. It's a matter of jealousy towards his work, not because he shares any relationship with these clones, or with Rei

Article: Ritsukos emotional state after the SEELE incident
 thing, I think, applies to Ritsuko. She hates the clones because Gendo values them more than he does her. It's a matter of jealousy towards his work, not because he shares any relationship with these clones, or with Rei 2 for that matter (which is more of a father/ daughter thing anyway). Ritsuko is a human, and humans tend to get jealous when something interferes with the attention they desire. To explain the point, let's set up a scenario: A multi-millionaire businessman and his wife live together with immeasurable riches. They live happily together, but the businessman is away for months at a time to make his living. While the wife doesn't have an affair with anyone else and she's well off, she still hates the fact that her husband is preoccupied with his work which is essential to their lifestyle. The wife tries to keep her husband at home for as long as possible until she's tired of living without him and leaves him for someone else. The point is, despite the riches or the importance of the work, humans want the full attention of their significant other, and are willing to live with less of anything if it means they get to see their significant other more often. Ritsuko basically thought "As much as we need this work to ensure survival, I want Gendo to be with me more." I think it's even possible to argue that as level as she appeared to be, she just repressed her desires to be loved by Gendo just because she knew the importance of the work. However, after the SEELE incident, she just couldn't keep it together and she wanted to send a message to Gendo just to say "Hey, why aren't you paying attention to me?! I should be more important than your work!" But this repression also applies to anything else she does throughout Evangelion; she's very subservient to Gendo at the beginning, but as everything fell apart, Ritsuko steps out of the subservient shell and is independently acting of her own will. Now, to conclude her reasoning behind the destruction of the clones, it goes back to her being a woman. While she wanted Gendo's attention, she also was jealous that the soulless abominations took more precedence than she did. After the SEELE incident, her mentality was probably "How did these clones win out to me? I'm an actual human and they can't even compare to me. Gendo just threw me to to dogs when he could have

Article: Ritsukos emotional state after the SEELE incident
 than she did. After the SEELE incident, her mentality was probably "How did these clones win out to me? I'm an actual human and they can't even compare to me. Gendo just threw me to to dogs when he could have sacrificed one of these shells to SEELE." Being further humiliated by Gendo by being sacrificed to SEELE without so much as a second glance probably set her over the edge. The killing of the clones then would have made sense, not to satisfy a desire to murder Rei, but as revenge for the importance they had over her. In my opinion, Shinji was brought into NERV not just because he was ordered to pilot Eva 01 by his father. I mean, it is the MAIN reason, because he wanted his father to approve of him, but I think every major character who understood NERV's practices had their own reason for it. Ritsuko isn't the type of person I think who would want to use Shinji to kill Gendo by inciting him, even though he may have wanted to because Gendo left him on the streets at 5. She doesn't even seem to have any intention of doing it, considering she is with him secretly for a good part of the series and could do it herself. No, other than to show NERV's practices to Shinji and to shed light on the organization, it was to attack Gendo emotionally (though this is probably a vain effort on her part). Ritsuko could send a message to Gendo emotionally by bringing Shinji to him saying "You can't just recluse yourself in your work. There are people here who care about and you know you care about and you just can't keep shutting them out.", which would work exceptionally well for Ritsuko's situation and her desired use of subterfuge to achieve her goals. I think everything else that lilim touched on I agree with, but I just thought I'd share my thoughts on the issue. But another theory that I was thinking about (though it may not make much sense) is that Ritsuko could perhaps have blamed Rei for her mother's death, even though she took her own life. I would think no matter what happened to a recently deceased sibling, anything that had to do with him or her, you automatically associate a hatred for that object or thing, because to you it brought about their death. Even though Ritsuko hated her mother and didn't want to be like her (we all know how well THAT went),

Article: Ritsukos emotional state after the SEELE incident
 with him or her, you automatically associate a hatred for that object or thing, because to you it brought about their death. Even though Ritsuko hated her mother and didn't want to be like her (we all know how well THAT went), she would probably feel like Rei was the reason she died, and she was old enough to have a vivid memory of her death. I'll admit that the issue seems flawed, but to some extent it makes sense because the mental trauma that came with the SEELE incident could have also released her association with Rei to her mother's death, assuming the whole mother flashback came to her after the SEELE incident and before the killing of the clones (I honestly don't remember what happened first). It's obvioulsy not canon, I just wanted to share an opinion on it.
